tasks.description: Separate intentional relational write guard rails from internal invariant failures in `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.ExecuteWriteGuardRails`. Acceptance criteria: descriptor writes and missing non-descriptor write-plan guard rails still return deterministic request-scoped `UnknownFailure` messages, but internal `InvalidOperationException` failures from target-context resolution, `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet`, and `RelationalWriteFlattener` are no longer converted into raw client-visible error text and instead flow through centralized exception logging with the generic 500 server-error body.
steps_to_verify: Add repository tests proving descriptor-write and missing-write-plan guard rails still return the current deterministic failure messages.
Add repository tests proving an internal `InvalidOperationException` from the flattener and from target-context resolution is not remapped by the repository catch block.
Exercise the handler or middleware path and confirm the error is logged with exception details while the client receives the generic server-error response instead of the raw exception message.

tasks.description: Add PUT create-new seam coverage for non-existent documents. Acceptance criteria: when `ResolveForPutAsync` returns `RelationalWriteTargetContext.CreateNew`, the relational PUT seam still reaches flattening and terminal handoff, the flattened root row carries `UnresolvedRootDocumentId`, and the flow remains on the PUT/update contract rather than switching to POST/upsert semantics.
steps_to_verify: Add a `RelationalWriteTargetContextResolver` test proving `ResolveForPutAsync` returns `CreateNew` when the document lookup returns no rows.

Add a repository or seam test that stubs `CreateNew` for PUT and asserts `FlatteningInput.OperationKind` is `Put`, `TargetContext` is `CreateNew`, and the flattened root row starts with `FlattenedWriteValue.UnresolvedRootDocumentId.Instance`.
Keep the terminal-stage expectation on the PUT/update path and verify the response matches the current DMS-983 PUT contract instead of asserting a POST-style 201 insert result.

tasks.description: Add a flattener regression test for `HasBoundScopeData` with deeply nested all-array extension scopes. Acceptance criteria: nested extension shapes that contain only objects and empty arrays, with no bound scalar, FK, or precomputed values and no emitted child collection candidates, do not produce root-extension rows or collection-aligned extension scope rows.
steps_to_verify: Add a root `_ext` test using a shape like `{ "_ext": { "sample": { "nested": { "onlyArrays": [] } } } }` and assert no root-extension rows are emitted.
Add a collection-aligned `_ext` variant under `$.addresses[*]._ext.sample` with the same all-array nesting and assert no attached aligned scope rows are emitted.
Run the relational flattener unit tests covering extension emission and confirm the new edge cases pass.

tasks.description: Remove or complete the empty XML documentation comment on `FlattenedWriteContractSupport`. Acceptance criteria: `RelationalWriteContracts.cs` no longer contains an empty or unterminated `<summary>` block above `FlattenedWriteContractSupport`.
steps_to_verify: Replace the empty XML doc with a short valid summary or delete it entirely.
Format the touched file if needed.
Inspect the file or build the backend project to confirm the malformed XML documentation comment is gone.
